message:
fix(rate_limit): enforce FIFO fairness by processing queue before fast-path acquire

try_acquire now checks pending_queue_count first, and if nonzero, runs
process_queue to grant or expire queued waiters before allowing a new
fast-path caller to consume refilled tokens. This prevents starvation
where burst callers repeatedly bypass queued waiters that have been
waiting for capacity.

The double-check pattern (check count → process_queue → re-check count)
ensures that timed-out queue entries do not permanently block the fast
path either: once the queue is drained of expired entries, try_acquire
proceeds normally.

Two new tests verify both sides:
- timed-out queue entries get cleared before rejecting fast-path
- queued waiters receive grants before a new fast-path caller

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
